name: finishing-a-development-branch
description: Git branch completion workflow. Use when implementation is complete, tests pass, and a feature branch needs to be integrated via merge, pull request, or cleanup.
capabilities: Detects test runner from project files and runs tests
Identifies the correct base branch for merge
Presents four options: merge locally, create PR, preserve, or discard
Executes Git operations with safety guards and confirmations
author: CodingCossack
securitySummary: This is a legitimate Git branch completion workflow skill. All 74 static findings are false positives: the analyzer incorrectly flagged 'git' commands as cryptographic operations, standard shell command capture for git output as injection risks, and routine output redirections as filesystem access. The skill performs standard git operations (checkout, merge, branch deletion, worktree cleanup) with proper safeguards including test verification, merge conflict detection, and user confirmations before destructive actions.
valueStatement: Managing feature branch completion manually takes time and risks errors. This skill automates the workflow for merging, creating pull requests, preserving, or discarding branches with built-in test verification and safety checks.
limitations: Does not resolve merge conflicts automatically
Requires GitHub CLI installed for automated PR creation
Does not force-push without explicit user request

useCases.title: Complete feature development
useCases.description: Integrate finished feature branches into main line with merge or pull request.

useCases.title: Standardize branch cleanup
useCases.description: Enforce consistent branch completion workflow across teams with safety checks.

useCases.title: End-of-task branch handling
useCases.description: Clean up worktrees and branches after AI-assisted implementation tasks complete.

promptTemplates.title: Basic merge
promptTemplates.prompt: Use finishing-a-development-branch to merge this feature branch into main.

promptTemplates.title: Create pull request
promptTemplates.prompt: Use finishing-a-development-branch to push this branch and create a pull request on GitHub.

promptTemplates.title: Discard with confirmation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use finishing-a-development-branch to discard this experimental branch after I confirm.

promptTemplates.title: Worktree cleanup
promptTemplates.prompt: The feature is merged. Use finishing-a-development-branch to clean up the worktree.

bestPractices: Always run tests before merging to catch issues early
Verify the correct base branch to avoid unintended merges
Use the preserve option when worktree needs to stay for review feedback
antiPatterns: Using force delete on branches without confirming
Merging without identifying the correct target branch

faq.question: Does this skill automatically resolve merge conflicts?
faq.answer: No. If merge conflicts occur, the skill reports BLOCKED:CONFLICTS and stops. You must resolve conflicts manually.

faq.question: Can I use this without GitHub CLI installed?
faq.answer: Yes. You can still merge locally or preserve the branch. PR creation will prompt you to install gh CLI or create PR manually.

faq.question: What happens to my worktree after merge?
faq.answer: For merge and discard options, the worktree is cleaned up. For PR creation and preserve, the worktree stays intact.

faq.question: Does this skill force-push branches?
faq.answer: No. The skill never force-pushes without explicit user request. It uses safe git operations.

faq.question: How does it determine the base branch?
faq.answer: It checks main, master, and develop branches for the most recent merge-base with the current branch.

faq.question: What test runners are supported?
faq.answer: npm/yarn, cargo, pytest, go test, and make test. The skill detects your project type automatically.
